title: platform-user — tenancy hooks
description: This module publishes NO tenancy hooks. Deliberate — platform_users is CROSS-TENANT by construction (see tenancy-columns.md §2 exception + design.md §4.3). The `#[AsTenancyHook]` mechanism (from the tenancy substrate) fires on every tenant-init to set request-scoped state (log context, cache prefix, tenant-aware config resolution). PlatformUser rows do NOT participate in tenant-init because they have no `tenant_id` — the platform_admin guard is orthogonal to tenant resolution. The `platform.audit` middleware handles the platform-plane's own request-scoped state (correlation_id, actor stamping) via a different mechanism (a standard Laravel middleware, not a tenancy hook). This file exists for uniform module shape and to explicitly document the non-participation.
notes: Every reviewer + the `tenancy-compliance-auditor` agent expects this file to remain empty. A future PR that adds a tenancy hook here is a compliance violation — platform_users cannot be tenant-scoped.
The equivalent request-scoped state platform-user needs (audit correlation, actor stamping) is set by the `platform.audit` middleware. See middleware.json.

There is exactly ONE case where a tenant-init would touch platform_users indirectly: when a super_admin is impersonating a tenant User (Wave 1b `access/delegation`). The impersonation session sets the tenant context; the platform_user_id of the impersonator is captured on the impersonation_sessions row + on every audit row via the correlation_id, but the tenant-init hook itself never traverses to platform_users.
